skill.name: cognitive-twin
skill.description: Continuous business health monitoring across 13 business domains. Scores domain health, generates periodic digests, detects anomalies, simulates decisions, and provides early warning signals for problems.
skill.summary: Monitor business health across 13 domains with scoring, digests, anomaly flags, simulations, and trend alerts.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences, words such as digest and designer, and a business stress-test phrase. Manual review found no executable commands, weak cryptographic use, network reconnaissance, prompt injection, or data exfiltration. The skill is documentation-only, so remaining risk is low and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The static command-execution hits are fenced text examples and command-reference prompts, not Ruby backtick execution or runnable shell code. No evidence found of scripts, command invocation, dynamic execution, or user-controlled shell arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown fenced blocks containing sample business reports and prompt labels. There is no executable file, command syntax, or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits are false positives from ordinary words and business content, including periodic digests, warning signals, and designer. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, DES, MD5, SHA-1, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is business reporting text, not security code. The skill contains no implementation that could perform weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Finding Is Business Stress Testing
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance hit is a false positive on a command-reference phrase for business stress testing. No evidence found of port scanning, host discovery, network requests, or reconnaissance tooling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 640 describes stress testing a business scenario, not a network. Nearby lines discuss economic downturns and key-person risk.

content.user_title: Monitor Business Health With Cognitive Twin
content.value_statement: Business leaders often miss early signals across finance, customers, product, team, and market conditions. This skill structures health checks, digests, anomaly reviews, simulations, and alerts across 13 business domains.
actual_capabilities: Scores business health across financial, customer, product, team, market, operations, and related domains.
Creates daily, weekly, and monthly business health digest formats.
Flags anomalies from supplied metrics and explains likely causes and actions.
Simulates decisions across domains, including hiring, runway, quality, and team impact.
Builds 30, 60, and 90 day trend reviews with inflection points and alerts.
limitations: Requires the user to provide current metrics and business context.
Does not include real-time API integrations in version 1.
Does not perform automated data ingestion from financial, CRM, HR, or analytics systems.
Does not replace executive judgment, accounting review, legal review, or board governance.

use_cases.title: Founder Weekly Health Review
use_cases.description: Turn startup metrics into a weekly health score, anomaly list, and focused action plan.

use_cases.title: Operator Monthly Business Digest
use_cases.description: Create a structured monthly review across domains for leadership meetings and planning.

use_cases.title: Investor Update Risk Scan
use_cases.description: Identify health trends, decision risks, and mitigation plans before preparing investor updates.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Health Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a business health check using these metrics: revenue, burn, runway, churn, NPS, product bugs, team satisfaction, and market notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Weekly Digest
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a weekly Cognitive Twin digest for my business. Include domain scores, anomalies, what is working, what needs attention, and next week focus.

prompt_templates.title: Simulate a Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Simulate this decision across financial, product, team, customer, and market domains: [decision]. Use a 90 day and 12 month view.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Strategic Risk Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these 12 months of business metrics. Identify trends, inflection points, cascading risks, alert thresholds, and a 30 day mitigation plan.

output_examples.input: Run a weekly health review for a startup with growing revenue, lower engagement, and declining team satisfaction.
output_examples.output: A domain scorecard, two anomaly explanations, a short strategic interpretation, and three prioritized actions for the next week.

output_examples.input: Simulate hiring an engineering manager with eight months of runway.
output_examples.output: A cross-domain tradeoff analysis showing runway cost, product quality upside, team impact, decision conditions, and recommended timing.

output_examples.input: Review 90 day trends for churn, runway, product engagement, and team morale.
output_examples.output: A trend outlook with positive signals, negative signals, likely inflection points, alert thresholds, and immediate mitigation steps.

best_practices: Provide recent metrics, comparison periods, and business stage before asking for a health score.
Review assumptions in every simulation before using the output for leadership decisions.
Track the same domain metrics over time so anomaly detection has a reliable baseline.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated scores as audited financial, legal, or compliance advice.
Do not ask for real-time monitoring unless connected systems and fresh data are available.
Do not hide uncertainty when metrics are incomplete, stale, or based on estimates.

faq.question: What does Cognitive Twin monitor?
faq.answer: It monitors 13 business domains, including finance, customers, product, team, market, operations, strategy, leadership, and culture.

faq.question: Does it connect to business systems automatically?
faq.answer: No. Version 1 documents analysis workflows and templates, but it does not include real-time API integrations.

faq.question: Can it create investor-ready summaries?
faq.answer: It can structure health insights, risks, trends, and actions that support investor updates.

faq.question: Can it replace business intelligence tools?
faq.answer: No. It helps interpret supplied metrics, but it does not collect, store, or validate source data.

faq.question: What data should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide revenue, burn, runway, churn, retention, NPS, product usage, uptime, bugs, hiring, morale, and market notes.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
